ground_truth_summary: 2x3 grid of 6 numbered animation frames showing a bright blue cartoon cat (pink inner ears, white muzzle, black nose) in a SINGLE RUN CYCLE (looping stride), NOT 6 distinct actions. Solid light blue background with simple flat shadow under each cat. Numbers 1-2-3 top row, 4-5-6 bottom row. The discriminator: models inventing 6 different unrelated actions (sitting, lying, sploot, etc.) are WRONG; models recognizing the looping run cycle are CORRECT.

strengths: Excellent layout and grid description (2x3, numbers 1-6 placement correct)
Good character description (blue fur, peach inner ears, white muzzle, black nose/eye)
Correctly identifies shadows under each cat
Correctly notes pale blue background and dark blue numbers
weaknesses: WRONG on the central discriminator: invents 6 DIFFERENT actions — running, landing, standing, crouching, sitting, 'sploot' lying flat — instead of recognizing the looping run cycle
The 'sploot' pose is a confabulation not visible in the frames
Frame-by-frame descriptions are fabricated rather than depicting a stride cycle
hallucinations_detected: Invents 'sploot' pose with paws tucked under chin (not in image)
Treats each frame as a distinct unrelated action rather than stride phases
Says cat is sitting on haunches in frame 5 — not accurate for a run cycle frame
judgments.summary: Strong on layout, color and OCR but fails the key discriminator — fabricates 6 distinct actions instead of recognizing the looping run cycle.

strengths: Correctly identifies 2x3 grid layout with numbers 1-6
Reasonable character description (cerulean blue body, pink inner ears)
Identifies pale blue background
Recognizes the cartoonish flat style
weaknesses: WRONG on the central discriminator: groups frames as '1&2 running', '3 walking', '4&5 sitting/crouching', '6 lying down' — fails to recognize the run cycle loop
Calls the cat 'kittens' (plural) instead of a single character in different poses
Frame grouping (1&2 running together, 4&5 sitting together) suggests separate actions, not a continuous stride
Says 'large expressive eyes' — eyes are actually small black dots
hallucinations_detected: Invents 'walking/stalking' as a separate action from running
Groups frames as distinct actions (running/walking/sitting/lying) instead of stride phases
'Large expressive eyes' — eyes are simple dots
judgments.summary: Gets layout and basic character design right, but misses the looping run cycle concept entirely, grouping frames into separate action categories.

strengths: BEST CORRECT interpretation: explicitly says 'sequential running motion (animation frames)' and 'progression from mid-stride through various stages to final running pose'
Recognizes it shows 'the order of the running sequence, emphasizing the animation flow'
Correctly notes light blue background and subtle blue shadows beneath each cat
Good character description: bright blue fur, pink inner ears, white muzzle, dark eyes
Clean layout description with top row 1-2-3 and bottom row 4-5-6
weaknesses: Frame-by-frame breakdown is less detailed than some competitors
Says 'final running pose (6)' — interpretation, but consistent with run cycle
judgments.summary: Best-in-class: explicitly identifies the sequential running animation and animation flow rather than inventing separate actions. Clean, accurate, no confabulations.

judgments.summary: Strong on layout, color, and character description; recognizes 'animation cycle' framing but per-frame descriptions drift toward separate actions rather than a clean run cycle.

Correctly notes 'Frame 6: similar to Frame 1, suggesting the cycle repeats' — perfectly captures the looping run cycle concept

aggregate_findings.best_run_cycle_recognition: qwen3-vl-8b-Q8|think explicitly states 'running animation cycle' and notes frame 6 mirrors frame 1 indicating the loop
aggregate_findings.second_best: glm-4.6v-flash-9b-Q8|think correctly identifies 'sequential running motion (animation frames)' and 'animation flow'
aggregate_findings.worst_run_cycle_recognition: qwen3.5-4b-Q8|think explicitly argues 'rather than a continuous circular run' — directly contradicts GT
common_hallucinations: 6 of 8 models drift toward describing distinct actions (sitting, lying, sploot, jumping) rather than the run cycle
Shadow colors frequently misdescribed: 'purple-blue gradient' or 'light gray' instead of simple blue
Frame 6 often misinterpreted as seated/lying rather than a stride phase mirroring frame 1
aggregate_findings.color_accuracies: Most models correctly identified the pale/light blue background; main errors were on shadow color (some said gray or purple)
aggregate_findings.layout_ocr: All 8 models correctly identified the 2x3 grid with numbers 1-6 — perfect on layout
